name: Indexed Finance
report: The attacker exploited the updateMinimumBalance function, which estimates the pool's total value using only a single token (e.g., UNI) via extrapolatePoolValueFromToken. By manipulating the pool’s asset composition—specifically draining UNI—the attacker caused the estimated pool value to be drastically underestimated. This led to a much lower minimumBalance for a newly added token (SUSHI), allowing the attacker to deposit a small amount of SUSHI, trigger gulp(), and receive an inflated weight, enabling massive arbitrage by minting index tokens cheaply and redeeming them for high-value assets.
